commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; maintainer review should decide whether to accept the stricter fail-closed create/revise contract before merge.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression found; the diff adds validation, docs, prompt/tool copy, and tests without dependency, workflow, secret, or permission changes. (sha=620718bb9a3932ca0865d8513f8a4eeb177d8c1c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a config-aware Skill Workshop create/revise guard that rejects create proposals whose markdown references existing workspace skill paths, plus prompt/tool/docs copy and focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +90, Tests +270, Docs +10. Total +370 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main creates a proposal from the submitted new skill name and does not inspect proposal markdown for existing `skills/<name>/...` targets; this read-only review did not run a live reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainer review should decide whether to accept the stricter fail-closed create/revise contract before merge.

target.title: fix(skills): guard workshop creates from existing skill targets
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #94947.

`skill_workshop` could accept `action=create` proposals whose markdown clearly targeted existing workspace skill paths, so applying the proposal created an unintended sibling skill instead of routing the work through the existing skill update path.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a create-proposal guard that scans proposal content for workspace-relative `skills/...` path references and rejects the create when those paths resolve inside any discovered workspace skill directory. The guard uses the existing workspace skill loader with the active OpenClaw config so direct skills, grouped skills such as `skills/group/nested-skill/SKILL.md`, non-canonical directory names, and configured trusted workspace symlink roots are checked against the actual loaded skill roots.

The same guard also runs when revising a pending create proposal, so a harmless create draft cannot later be revised into a wrong-target existing-skill patch.

## User Impact

Agents now get an immediate, actionable error when `action=create` content points at existing workspace skills. The tool and Skill Workshop docs tell agents to use `action=update` with `skill_name` and split multi-skill changes into separate update proposals.

## Evidence

- Behavior addressed: `action=create` now rejects both initial create proposals and later create-proposal revisions when proposal content names an existing workspace skill path, including direct skills, grouped/nested skills, non-canonical skill directory names, and configured trusted workspace symlink skill roots.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree with the repo's Codex-worktree Vitest wrapper, plus a redacted terminal proof that calls the real `skill_workshop` tool handler against a temporary workspace with `skills/` symlinked to a configured trusted target.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install`; `pnpm docs:list`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/workshop/create-target-guard.test.ts src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/tools/skill-workshop.md src/agents/skill-workshop-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.ts src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.test.ts src/skills/workshop/create-target-guard.ts src/skills/workshop/create-target-guard.test.ts src/skills/workshop/service.ts src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: `create-target-guard.test.ts` builds real workspace skill fixtures under `skills/foo/SKILL.md`, `skills/group/nested-skill/SKILL.md`, and `skills/foo_bar/SKILL.md` and verifies existing-skill path refs are rejected while unrelated/new/url/prose refs are allowed. It also verifies configured trusted symlink workspace roots are visible to the guard. `service.test.ts` verifies `proposeCreateSkill` rejects existing-skill refs before creating a proposal, and `reviseSkillProposal` rejects revisions that would redirect a pending create proposal to an existing workspace skill without advancing the proposal version, including the configured trusted symlink root case. `skill-workshop-tool.test.ts` verifies the public `skill_workshop` wrapper preserves that configured trusted symlink behavior for both create and revise.
- Observed result after fix: the focused Vitest command passed in 3 shards covering 4 files / 137 tests; diff whitespace and oxfmt checks passed.
- Real `skill_workshop` terminal proof, with temporary paths redacted:

```text
real skill_workshop proof: configured workspace skills symlink target
setup: workspace=<tmp>/workspace, skills -> <tmp>/trusted-skills, allowSymlinkTargets=[<tmp>/trusted-skills]
create existing target: rejected: action=create cannot propose changes to existing workspace skills: skills/shared-skill/. Use action=update with skill_name for each existing skill instead.
create safe proposal: ok: {"id":"new-helper-20260620-03f12af69b","status":"pending","kind":"create","skillName":"New Helper","skillKey":"new-helper","proposalFile":"PROPOSAL.md","supportFileCount":0,"targetSkillFile":"<tmp>/workspace/skills/new-helper/SKILL.md","scanState":"clean","proposedVersion":"v1"}
revise toward existing target: rejected: action=create cannot propose changes to existing workspace skills: skills/shared-skill/. Use action=update with skill_name for each existing skill instead.
post-revise inspect: status=pending version=v1
```

- What was not tested: no full Testbox gate or live end-to-end agent session was run; this is a narrow Skill Workshop service/tool/prompt/docs patch covered by focused unit/tool behavior tests, redacted terminal proof through the real tool handler, and GitHub's external-PR proof check.
- Review proof: one Codex autoreview round found two P2 issues before this draft PR; both were fixed. A later ClawSweeper review requested config-aware trusted symlink coverage plus real tool-path output; this update adds both. Per local OpenClaw guardrails, no second Codex autoreview loop was run for draft PR work.
target.author: monkeyleet

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
